question: Suppose a screening test has a sensitivity of 0.98 and a false-positive rate of 0.01 . The test is used in a population that has a disease prevalence of 0.001 .
answer:
We can use Bayes' theorem to determine the predictive value of a positive test under these circumstances as follows

$$
 egin{aligned}
\operatorname{Pr}(D \mid T) & = rac{	ext { sensitivity × prevalence }}{[	ext { sensitivity × prevalence }]+[	ext { false positive rate } 	imes(1-	ext { prevalence })]} \
& = rac{0.98 	imes 0.001}{[0.98 	imes 0.001]+[0.01 	imes(1-0.001)]}=0.0893
\end{aligned}
$$
The probability of having disease given a positive test result is $8.93 \%$.

meta info.caption: Predictive value positive.
meta info.section: 5.5  MORE RULES AND PROPERTIES OF PROBABILITY
